Explanation: Using `select()` without specifying columns fetches all columns from the CHAT table, which can retrieve unnecessary data and cause maintenance issues if the table schema changes. Explicitly list the required columns, e.g., `.select(CHAT.CHAT_ID, CHAT.NAME)`.

Using select() without specifying columns fetches all columns from the LINK table, which can retrieve unnecessary data and hurt performance. Explicitly list only the required columns, e.g., .select(LINK.LINK_ID, LINK.NAME, LINK.CUR_TIME, LINK.LAST_UPDATE).
